title: quercus sessiliflora var. microcarpa
intro: Quercus sessiliflora var. microcarpa, also known as the small-fruited evergreen oak, is a plant species within the Quercus genus. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora var. microcarpa include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
